summary:
• Venezuela's requirement that oil firms self-supply power threatens to further constrain refinery operations and crude production at a time when the country's output is already critically depleted.

• Middle East geopolitical escalation between Iran and US allies is creating persistent crude price volatility and supply uncertainty that could disrupt global markets if shipping routes through the region face disruption.

• Argentina's major Vaca Muerta project approval and Delfin LNG's $5 billion FID signal potential supply growth from South America, but these developments face execution risks and will take years to materially impact hemispheric crude availability.
